findings.title: Authentication disabled by default (empty api_token)
findings.description: api_token defaults to empty string. Auth middleware passes all requests when token is empty (auth.rs:33-35). All 38 API endpoints + WebSocket are unauthenticated by default.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Attacker discovers OpenStory on network (port 3002). 2. GET /api/sessions returns all sessions. 3. GET /api/sessions/{id}/transcript returns full agent conversations. 4. DELETE /api/sessions/{id} destroys sessions. No credentials required.
findings.impact: Complete read/write access to all coding agent transcripts, file contents, tool inputs/outputs, and session metadata.
findings.recommendation: Make api_token required — fail startup if empty. Generate and print a random token on first startup.

findings.title: WebSocket authentication not implemented
findings.description: ws_handler accepts WebSocket upgrade without any token validation. Comment in auth.rs:5 claims '?token=' query param support but code does not implement it. Even if api_token is configured for REST, WebSocket is completely open.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Admin configures api_token for REST API auth. 2. Attacker connects to ws://target:3002/ws (no token needed). 3. Receives initial_state with all sessions' data (2000 records). 4. Streams all live events. REST API token becomes useless.
findings.impact: Complete bypass of REST API authentication via WebSocket. All session data, patterns, and real-time events accessible.
findings.recommendation: Extract ?token= query param in ws_handler. Validate against config.api_token before ws.on_upgrade(). Return 401 if missing/invalid.
findings.verification: independently verified — ws.rs:35-40 has no token logic

findings.title: Database unencrypted by default (db_key empty)
findings.description: db_key defaults to empty string. SQLite database at data/open-story.db stores all event data (transcripts, file contents, tool outputs) as plaintext on disk.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Attacker gains filesystem access (container escape, backup theft, shared machine). 2. Reads data/open-story.db directly. 3. All agent transcripts, file contents, and tool outputs exposed.
findings.impact: Complete exposure of all historical session data at rest.
findings.recommendation: Generate and persist a random db_key on first startup. Warn loudly if db_key is empty. Document SQLCipher encryption as mandatory for shared environments.

findings.title: Grafana default password in observability stack
findings.description: GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD=openstory hardcoded. Only in the observability overlay, not the default deployment.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Attacker accesses Grafana on the observability stack. 2. Logs in with admin/openstory. 3. Reads metrics dashboards, potentially modifies alert rules.
findings.impact: Grafana admin access on the observability overlay.
findings.recommendation: Use environment variable or Docker secret. Remove hardcoded password.

findings.title: NATS JetStream no authentication or TLS
findings.description: NATS runs with no authentication and no TLS. Anyone with network access can publish/subscribe to event streams.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Attacker on same network connects to nats://target:4222. 2. Subscribes to events.> to receive all session data. 3. Publishes fabricated events to inject false data.
findings.impact: Event eavesdropping, injection, and tampering on the NATS bus.
findings.recommendation: Enable NATS auth (authorization block with user/password or token). Enable TLS for production deployments.
findings.verification: independently verified — nats.conf has no auth/tls blocks

findings.title: Authentication failures not logged
findings.description: auth_middleware returns StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED with no logging on failed attempts. No audit trail for brute force detection.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Attacker brute-forces api_token. 2. No log entries generated. 3. Admin has no visibility into attack.
findings.impact: No audit trail for authentication attempts. Cannot detect brute force.
findings.recommendation: Add log_event() on auth failure. Include client IP (if available) and request path.
findings.verification: independently verified — auth.rs:50 returns Err with no logging
